content.problemStatement:
**N** one dimensional kingdoms are represented as intervals of the form **[ai , bi]** on the real line.
A kingdom of the form **[L, R]** can be destroyed completely by placing a bomb at a point **x** on the real line if **L 
≤ x ≤ R**.



Your task is to determine minimum number of bombs required to destroy all the one dimensional kingdoms.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N on the first line, followed by N lines, each containing two space-separated integers ai and bi representing the interval of the kingdom.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of bombs required to destroy all the one-dimensional kingdoms.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are three kingdoms [1,3], [2,5], and [6,9]. We will need at least 2 bombs to destroy the kingdoms. In one of the possible solutions, we can place two bombs at x = 2 and x = 6.
There are two kingdoms [1,2] and [3,4]. We will need at least 2 bombs to destroy the kingdoms. In one of the possible solutions, we can place two bombs at x = 1 and x = 3.
There is one kingdom [1,5]. We will need at least 1 bomb to destroy the kingdom. In one of the possible solutions, we can place one bomb at x = 1.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using a greedy algorithm. The idea is to sort the intervals based on their end points and then iterate through the sorted intervals. For each interval, if its start point is greater than the end point of the previous interval, we increment the count of bombs and update the end point of the previous interval.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N log N) per test case, O(T * N log N) total. The sorting step takes O(N log N) time, and the iteration through the sorted intervals takes O(N) time.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(sys.stdin.readline())
    intervals = [list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())) for _ in range(N)]
    intervals.sort(key=lambda x: x[1])
    min_bombs = 0
    last_bomb = -1
    for interval in intervals:
        if interval[0] > last_bomb:
            min_bombs += 1
            last_bomb = interval[1]
    print(min_bombs)
